An answer that makes reference to the following point: • ammonia / NH Allow NH OH Ignore references to concentration Do not award NH •H O (1) (Total for Question 4 = 7 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • letters in the correct order (2) All five in the correct order scores (2) Any two letters in the correct place (1) (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • correct equation • correct state symbols (1) (1) Example of an equation: NaHCO (aq) + HCl(aq) → NaCl(aq) +H O(l) + CO (g) Allow ionic equation HCO – (aq) + H + (aq) → H O(l) + CO (g) M2 dependent on M1 (or a near-miss) (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • to dry the (organic) liquid (1) Allow to absorb water Allow to act as a drying agent Allow dry the (organic) solution Allow “eliminate water” Ignore “remove water” alone Do not award dehydration (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark points: • alcohol X / /2-methylpropan-2-ol • (alcohol X) is tertiary / 3° • (alcohol X) will form a more stable carbocation (1) (1) (1) If X, the formula and the name are given all must be correct Allow forms a tertiary carbocation Ignore shape of carbocation Ignore comments on activation energy (3) Expert Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • heat (with sulfuric acid and potassium dichromate(VI)) • X will not turn green • distil the products (from Y and Z) • Z product will react with Fehling’s/Benedict’s • to give a red/brown precipitate (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow warm Ignore reflux for M1 when aldehydes are implied Do not award hydrochloric acid Accept Y and Z will produce a colour change orange to green Allow X will not produce a colour change from orange Accept converse for Y product Accept Z product will react with Tollens’ (1) to give a silver mirror (1) M5 has to have the correct reagent to be awarded “silver mirror” without Tollens would not score. Alternative: reflux (M1) M2 and M3 are the same as above Z product will react with CO 2− /HCO − (M4) To fizz / gas turns lime water milky/white (M5)
